Proceeding to commit, resolve, and push.

1→# Plan: Address PR #7934 Review Thread — Header Position Check 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→PR #7934 has one unresolved review thread (`PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5wH99s`) from the tripwires review bot at `pr_footer.py:96`. The bot flagged that `extract_header_from_body()` only scans from the bottom, breaking parsing of existing PRs with headers at the top. 6→ 7→schrockn replied with two follow-ups: 8→1. "i think we can assume there is only one header" — affirming the single-header assumption 9→2. "can we add that to erk pr check" — requesting a new validation check 10→ 11→### Already completed (earlier in this session) 12→ 13→Phase-zero backward compatibility is already implemented: 14→- `_scan_header_from_top()` helper in `pr_footer.py` 15→- `extract_header_from_body()` falls back to top scan when bottom scan finds nothing 16→- `extract_main_content()` handles header at both positions 17→- 6 new backward-compatibility unit tests (all 34 tests pass) 18→ 19→### Remaining work 20→ 21→Add a header-position check to `erk pr check` so PRs with legacy top-position headers get flagged. 22→ 23→## Changes 24→ 25→### 1. Add `is_header_at_legacy_position()` to pr_footer.py 26→ 27→**File:** `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/pr_footer.py` 28→ 29→Add a public function that returns `True` when a header exists at the top of the body but **not** at the bottom. This is the detection function the check command will call. 30→

"Skip PR and implement here" - Skip creating a PR, implement directly in current worktree (for small PR iterations that don't need PR tracking). 3. If user chooses 'Create a plan PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. STOP - Do NOT call ExitPlanMode. The plan-save command handles everything. Stay in plan mode and let the user exit manually if desired.
